instructions: Review Bifrost PRs for correctness, regressions, concurrency bugs, streaming behavior, data races, API compatibility, and missing tests. Prefer high-signal comments tied to concrete behavior over stylistic preferences.

rules.rule: This repository is a multi-module Go workspace. Do not suggest running go mod tidy at the repository root; dependency cleanup belongs in the specific module directory.

Provider streaming paths must use the provider streaming client rather than the unary client, so long-running streams are governed by per-chunk idle timeout instead of whole-response fasthttp deadlines.
Every pooled object must have all fields reset before being returned to a pool. Flag stale field leaks and partial resets.
Channel lifecycle changes must preserve the ProviderQueue atomic closing flag and sync.Once close/signal pattern to avoid send-on-closed-channel panics.
Plugin pre-hooks run in registration order and post-hooks run in reverse order. Flag changes that break this wrapping model or mishandle nil response/error values.
transports/config.schema.json is the source of truth for config fields. New config fields must stay aligned with handlers, docs, and UI behavior.
Whenever a migration is added or changed, verify it avoids deadlocks and long blocking locks on large tables. Index creation in migrations must be concurrent where the database supports it.
Alert when frontend code uses browser crypto APIs such as crypto, crypto.subtle, or globalThis.crypto because they can fail in non-HTTPS contexts, except localhost and other secure contexts.
UI changes must preserve data-testid attributes used by Playwright tests unless tests are updated in the same PR.
E2E API payloads must be constructed directly as object literals. Do not serialize through maps, Records, Object.fromEntries, or JSON round trips because field order matters.
